error.signature: RuntimeError: element 0 of tensors does not require grad and does not have a grad_fn
error.regex: (does not require grad|does not have a grad_fn|element 0 of tensors|can't compute gradient|grad can be implicitly created only for scalar)

verdict.summary: Calling backward() on a tensor that is detached from the computation graph. Tensor was created without requires_grad or was detached.

dead_ends.action: Set requires_grad=True on the loss tensor directly
dead_ends.why_fails: Loss should inherit grad_fn from model parameters via forward pass; setting it manually breaks the chain

dead_ends.action: Use .data attribute to access tensor values
dead_ends.why_fails: .data detaches the tensor from autograd; operations on .data do not build a computation graph

workarounds.how: for p in model.parameters(): print(p.requires_grad)  # should all be True; check if optimizer was set up

workarounds.action: Verify the forward pass builds a computation graph
workarounds.how: output = model(input); print(output.grad_fn)  # should not be None; check for .detach() or .item() in forward

workarounds.action: Check that torch.no_grad() context is not wrapping the training loop
workarounds.how: with torch.no_grad(): is for inference only; remove it from training code
